_meta.wait_gate: submit ONLY after BOTH Framework (draft 01) and AVR Score (draft 02) are accepted AND stable for 30+ days
_meta.notability_criterion: Criterion 3 (structural need) IF anchored to accepted Framework + AVR Score; otherwise no criterion satisfied
_meta.risk_level: HIGH (concept is novel, only one source, no third-party adoption)
_meta.anchors_to: AI Visibility Readiness Framework (draft 01) + AVR Score (draft 02)
_meta.OPERATOR_NOTE: This is the riskiest of the three drafts. Pre-staged per operator request but submission is strongly discouraged until terminology adoption can be demonstrated (cite-back-tracking-spec metric shows 'calibration receipt' appears in third-party content).

descriptions.en: Timestamped forensic record of one AI-engine query response containing model version, raw verbatim output, citation extraction, and replay-safe hash; the unit of evidence in the AI Visibility Readiness Framework

statements.label: instance of

value_qid_options.label: concept
value_qid_options.note: concept is the safest catch-all if no better fit exists

value_qid_options.label: data structure
value_qid_options.note: if framing it as a technical artifact rather than a conceptual abstraction

statements.operator_decision: pick ONE (default Q1437361 concept; Q189290 if the operator wants technical-artifact framing)

statements.label: part of
statements.value_qid_pending: <Q-ID of AI Visibility Readiness Framework, once draft 01 is accepted>

statements.label: has part
statements.note: Conceptually, a calibration receipt has parts (timestamp, model version, raw output, citation extraction, hash) but each part is not Wikidata-worthy on its own. SKIP.

statements.label: official website
statements.note: citability.dev operationalizes the AVR Framework and is the canonical implementation reference for calibration receipts. If citability.dev/docs does not yet exist as a page, fall back to https://citability.dev or use https://chudi.dev/framework.

all.label: reference URL
all.note: AVR Framework spec defines calibration receipt as the per-query unit of evidence

all.label: reference URL
all.note: citability.dev methodology documentation; VERIFY this URL exists and contains a calibration receipt section before submission

operator_pre_submit_checklist: STRONGLY RECOMMENDED: defer indefinitely until cite-back-tracking-spec metric shows 'calibration receipt' appearing in third-party content (competitor listicles, academic papers, vendor blogs). This is the deletion-protection signal.
If proceeding anyway: verify https://citability.dev/docs exists and contains calibration receipt definition; create the page first if not.
Pick ONE primary P31 from {Q1437361, Q186506, Q189290}; default Q1437361 (concept).
Fill P361 part-of value with Framework Q-ID (depends on draft 01 acceptance).

Consider waiting until calibration receipt is referenced from at least ONE academic paper or recognized industry blog before submitting; without external adoption, this Q-item is the most likely of the three to be deletion-nominated.
